---
title: "MaAsLiN3 CANOE Workflow"
author: "Elad Deiss-Yehiely"
date: "2026-01-06"
output: html_document
---

#Learning MaAsLiN for differential abundance based on primary outcomes
```{r}
library(maaslin3)
library(phyloseq)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)


# ===========================================================================
# 1.  LOAD PHYLOSEQ & EXTRACT DATA
# ===========================================================================
 
physeq <- readRDS("/Users/ediessy/Desktop/phy_prev_10O_10R.rds")
 
# -- Feature table (rows = samples, columns = ASVs) --------------------------
feat_table <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq))
cat("Feature table dimensions:", dim(feat_table), "\n")
# Should be samples x ASVs (e.g. 883 x 118)
 
# -- Metadata ----------------------------------------------------------------
meta <- data.frame(sample_data(physeq), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
 
# -- Add sequencing depth ----------------------------------------------------
meta$sequencing_depth <- sample_sums(physeq)
 
# -- Define key variable names -----------------------------------------------
OUTCOME    <- "chronic_rhinitis24m"
SITE       <- "canoe_site.x"
SEQDEPTH   <- "sequencing_depth"
SUBJECT_ID <- "Subject_ID"
 
# -- Convert logical outcome to proper factor ---------------------------------
#    Check what it looks like before conversion
cat("\nOutcome before conversion:\n")
print(table(meta[[OUTCOME]], useNA = "always"))
 
meta[[OUTCOME]] <- factor(
  meta[[OUTCOME]],
  levels = c(FALSE, TRUE),
  labels = c("No", "Yes")
)
 
#    Verify conversion worked
cat("\nOutcome after conversion:\n")
print(table(meta[[OUTCOME]], useNA = "always"))
# Should show counts for No, Yes, and <NA>
 
# -- Set reference level for site --------------------------------------------
meta[[SITE]] <- factor(meta[[SITE]],
                       levels = c("UWIM", "HFHS", "VAUN", "WUSL"))
 
# -- Force clean data.frame --------------------------------------------------
meta <- data.frame(lapply(meta, function(x) {
  if (is.data.frame(x)) x[[1]] else x
}), row.names = rownames(meta))
 
# -- Sanity checks -----------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(all(rownames(feat_table) == rownames(meta)))
cat("\nSamples:", nrow(meta), "\n")
cat("ASVs   :", ncol(feat_table), "\n")
cat("Sequencing depth range:", range(meta$sequencing_depth), "\n")
cat("\nKey variable types:\n")
str(meta[, c(OUTCOME, SITE, SEQDEPTH, SUBJECT_ID)])
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 2.  TAXONOMY LOOKUP
# ===========================================================================
 
tax_lookup <- as.data.frame(tax_table(physeq)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("feature") %>%
  mutate(
    tax_label = case_when(
      !is.na(Genus)  & Genus  != "" ~ Genus,
      !is.na(Family) & Family != "" ~ paste0(Family, " (Family)"),
      !is.na(Order)  & Order  != "" ~ paste0(Order,  " (Order)"),
      TRUE                          ~ feature
    )
  ) %>%
  select(feature, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, tax_label)
 
# Check taxonomy column names match yours
cat("\nTaxonomy ranks available:", colnames(tax_table(physeq)), "\n")
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 3.  RUN MaAsLiN3 — TSS + LOG
# ===========================================================================
 
fit_TSS <- maaslin3(
  input_data     = feat_table,
  input_metadata = meta,
  output         = "/Users/ediessy/Desktop/Postdoc UCSF/CANOE/MaAsLiN3 Loop/maaslin3_TSS_chronic_rhinitis24m",
  formula        = paste0("~ ", OUTCOME, " + ", SEQDEPTH, " + ", SITE,
                          " + (1|", SUBJECT_ID, ")"),
  normalization  = "TSS",
  transform      = "LOG",
  augment        = TRUE,
  standardize    = TRUE,
  max_significance             = 0.1,
  median_comparison_abundance  = TRUE,
  median_comparison_prevalence = FALSE,
  save_models    = FALSE
)
 
# -- Check what metadata values MaAsLiN3 actually produced ------------------
cat("\nMetadata values in TSS abundance results:\n")
print(unique(fit_TSS$fit_data_abundance$results$metadata))
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 4.  RUN MaAsLiN3 — CLR
# ===========================================================================
 
fit_CLR <- maaslin3(
  input_data     = feat_table,
  input_metadata = meta,
  output         = "/Users/ediessy/Desktop/Postdoc UCSF/CANOE/MaAsLiN3 Loop/maaslin3_CLR_chronic_rhinitis24m",
  formula        = paste0("~ ", OUTCOME, " + ", SEQDEPTH, " + ", SITE,
                          " + (1|", SUBJECT_ID, ")"),
  normalization  = "CLR",
  transform      = "NONE",
  augment        = TRUE,
  standardize    = TRUE,
  max_significance             = 0.1,
  median_comparison_abundance  = FALSE,
  median_comparison_prevalence = FALSE,
  warn_prevalence              = FALSE,
  save_models    = FALSE
)
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 5.  EXTRACT RESULTS
#     We check what the metadata column actually looks like FIRST,
#     then build the filter around that.
# ===========================================================================
 
# -- Inspect the actual metadata string for our outcome ----------------------
outcome_metadata_values <- fit_TSS$fit_data_abundance$results %>%
  filter(str_detect(metadata, OUTCOME)) %>%
  pull(metadata) %>%
  unique()
 
cat("\nMetadata rows matching outcome variable:\n")
print(outcome_metadata_values)
# This tells us the exact string to filter on (e.g. "chronic_rhinitis24mYes")
 
# -- Build filter string dynamically based on what MaAsLiN3 actually used ---
#    If MaAsLiN3 appended "Yes" use that; if just the variable name, use that
outcome_filter <- if (length(outcome_metadata_values) > 0) {
  outcome_metadata_values  # use whatever MaAsLiN3 actually produced
} else {
  paste0(OUTCOME, "Yes")   # fallback
}
 
cat("\nWill filter on:", outcome_filter, "\n")
 
# -- Extract function --------------------------------------------------------
extract_outcome_results <- function(fit_out, outcome_filter, norm_label,
                                    outcome_var) {
 
  abund <- fit_out$fit_data_abundance$results %>%
    filter(metadata %in% outcome_filter) %>%
    mutate(model = "Abundance", norm = norm_label, outcome = outcome_var)
 
  prev <- fit_out$fit_data_prevalence$results %>%
    filter(metadata %in% outcome_filter) %>%
    mutate(model = "Prevalence", norm = norm_label, outcome = outcome_var)
 
  bind_rows(abund, prev)
}
 
res_TSS <- extract_outcome_results(fit_TSS, outcome_filter, "TSS + LOG", OUTCOME)
res_CLR <- extract_outcome_results(fit_CLR, outcome_filter, "CLR",       OUTCOME)
 
cat("\nRows extracted — TSS:", nrow(res_TSS), " CLR:", nrow(res_CLR), "\n")
 
# -- Combine and add taxonomy ------------------------------------------------
res_all <- bind_rows(res_TSS, res_CLR) %>%
  left_join(tax_lookup, by = "feature")

# Step 1 — identify which tax_labels have multiple ASVs
multi_asv_labels <- tax_lookup %>%
  group_by(tax_label) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(feature) > 1) %>%
  pull(tax_label) %>%
  unique()

# Step 2 — apply per-row unique label (no group_by needed)
res_all <- res_all %>%
  mutate(
    tax_label_unique = ifelse(
      tax_label %in% multi_asv_labels,
      paste0(tax_label, " (", feature, ")"),
      tax_label
    )
  )

cat("Total rows in res_all:", nrow(res_all), "\n")
 
# -- Quick look at significant hits ------------------------------------------
res_all %>%
  filter(qval_individual < 0.1) %>%
  select(norm, model, feature, tax_label, coef, qval_individual) %>%
  arrange(norm, model, qval_individual) %>%
  print(n = 30)
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 6.  CONCORDANCE TABLE (TSS vs CLR)
# ===========================================================================
 
concordance_table <- res_all %>%
  filter(model == "Abundance") %>%
  select(feature, tax_label, norm, coef, pval_individual, qval_individual) %>%
  mutate(norm = recode(norm, "TSS + LOG" = "TSS")) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = norm,
    values_from = c(coef, pval_individual, qval_individual),
    names_sep   = "_"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    concordant_direction = sign(coef_TSS) == sign(coef_CLR),
    CLR_nominal_sig      = pval_individual_CLR < 0.05,
    confidence_tier      = case_when(
      concordant_direction & CLR_nominal_sig  ~ "High",
      concordant_direction & !CLR_nominal_sig ~ "Moderate",
      !concordant_direction                   ~ "Low"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(qval_individual_TSS < 0.1) %>%
  arrange(confidence_tier, qval_individual_TSS)
 
cat("\nConcordance table:\n")
print(concordance_table)
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 7.  VOLCANO PLOT FUNCTION
# ===========================================================================
 
make_volcano <- function(df,
                         title       = "Volcano Plot",
                         q_threshold = 0.1,
                         top_n_label = 15) {
 
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      neg_log10_q = -log10(qval_individual + 1e-10),
      direction   = case_when(
        qval_individual < q_threshold & coef > 0 ~ "Up (significant)",
        qval_individual < q_threshold & coef < 0 ~ "Down (significant)",
        TRUE                                      ~ "Not significant"
      ),
      direction = factor(direction,
                         levels = c("Up (significant)",
                                    "Down (significant)",
                                    "Not significant"))
    )
 
  top_hits <- df %>%
    filter(qval_individual < q_threshold) %>%
    arrange(qval_individual) %>%
    slice_head(n = top_n_label)
 
  ggplot(df, aes(x = coef, y = neg_log10_q,
                 color = direction, label = tax_label_unique)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(q_threshold),
               linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
               linetype = "solid", color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_text_repel(data          = top_hits,
                    size          = 2.8,
                    max.overlaps  = 20,
                    box.padding   = 0.4,
                    segment.color = "grey50") +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Up (significant)"   = "#D73027",
                 "Down (significant)" = "#4575B4",
                 "Not significant"    = "grey70"),
      name = NULL
    ) +
    labs(title   = title,
         x       = "MaAsLiN3 coefficient (effect size)",
         y       = expression(-log[10](q[individual])),
         caption = paste0("q_individual < ", q_threshold, " (BH FDR)")) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
          legend.position = "bottom")
}
 
 
# ===========================================================================
# 8.  BUILD & SAVE 2x2 COMPOSITE VOLCANO
# ===========================================================================
 
v_TSS_abund <- res_all %>%
  filter(norm == "TSS + LOG", model == "Abundance") %>%
  make_volcano(title = "TSS + LOG  |  Abundance")

v_TSS_prev <- res_all %>%
  filter(norm == "TSS + LOG", model == "Prevalence") %>%
  make_volcano(title = "TSS + LOG  |  Prevalence")

v_CLR_abund <- res_all %>%
  filter(norm == "CLR", model == "Abundance") %>%
  make_volcano(title = "CLR  |  Abundance")

v_CLR_prev <- res_all %>%
  filter(norm == "CLR", model == "Prevalence") %>%
  make_volcano(title = "CLR  |  Prevalence")

composite_plot <- (v_TSS_abund | v_TSS_prev) /
                  (v_CLR_abund  | v_CLR_prev) +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Chronic Rhinitis at 24 Months",
    subtitle = "MaAsLiN3 — TSS+LOG vs CLR, Abundance vs Prevalence"
  )

save_path <- "/Users/ediessy/Desktop"

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(save_path, "volcano_2x2_chronic_rhinitis24m_FIXED.pdf"),
  plot     = composite_plot,
  width    = 14, height = 11
)

cat("Plot saved!\n")


# ===========================================================================
# 9.  SAVE RESULTS
# ===========================================================================

write.csv(res_all,
          file      = file.path(save_path, "res_all_chronic_rhinitis24m.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(concordance_table,
          file      = file.path(save_path, "concordance_chronic_rhinitis24m.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

cat("Results saved!\n")


# ===========================================================================
# 10.  OPTIONAL — QUICK SUMMARY OF SIGNIFICANT HITS
# ===========================================================================
 
res_all %>%
  filter(qval_individual < 0.1) %>%
  select(norm, model, feature, coef, stderr, pval_individual, qval_individual) %>%
  arrange(norm, model, qval_individual) %>%
  print(n = 50)
 
 
###############################################################################
# QUICK REFERENCE: KEY MaAsLiN3 PARAMETERS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# normalization:  "TSS"  = total-sum scaling (proportions)      [recommended]
#                 "CLR"  = centred log-ratio (handles compositionality)
#                 "NONE" = use if data already normalised externally
#
# transform:      "LOG"  = log2 (use with TSS)                  [recommended]
#                 "NONE" = no transform (use with CLR — it already log-transforms)
#                 "PLOG" = pseudo-log (useful for metabolomics)
#
# median_comparison_abundance:
#                 TRUE   = subtract median coefficient across features
#                          (compositional correction — use with TSS)
#                 FALSE  = no median subtraction (use with CLR)
#
# augment:        TRUE   = adds small pseudo-observations to logistic model
#                          to avoid linear separability         [recommended]
#
# standardize:    TRUE   = z-score continuous covariates so coefficients
#                          are on the same scale                [recommended]
#
# max_significance: FDR (q-value) cutoff for "significant"     [default 0.1]
###############################################################################

```
